title: Hibernate
description: Hibernate and JPA: entity lifecycle, persistence context, fetching strategies and common pitfalls.

questions.question: What is the relationship between JPA and Hibernate? Explain the roles of EntityManager, the persistence context and the first-level cache.
Describe the entity lifecycle states in Hibernate (transient, managed/persistent, detached, removed). What is dirty checking and when does Hibernate actually flush changes to the database?
What is the N+1 select problem? Show how it arises with lazy-loaded associations and describe at least two ways to fix it (e.g. fetch join, @EntityGraph, batch fetching).
Compare LAZY and EAGER fetching. Why is EAGER on collections considered harmful, and what causes a LazyInitializationException? Why is the Open Session in View pattern controversial?
How does Hibernate handle concurrent modifications? Compare optimistic locking with @Version against pessimistic locking, and explain what happens when an OptimisticLockException is thrown.

questions: What is the difference between EntityManager.find() and getReference() (or Hibernate's get() vs load())? When does each hit the database, and when would a proxy returned by getReference() throw an exception?

Explain the common identifier generation strategies (IDENTITY, SEQUENCE, TABLE, AUTO, UUID). Why does IDENTITY prevent JDBC batching of inserts, and which strategy would you pick for a high-throughput PostgreSQL application?
How should you implement equals() and hashCode() for JPA entities? Explain the problems with using the database-generated id or all fields, and how proxies and detached entities complicate this.
How do you correctly map a bidirectional @OneToMany/@ManyToOne association? Explain what mappedBy means, which side is the owning side, and why helper methods that sync both sides are recommended.

What do the JPA cascade types (PERSIST, MERGE, REMOVE, etc.) do, and how is orphanRemoval = true different from CascadeType.REMOVE? Give an example where using the wrong one deletes data you wanted to keep.

What is the difference between merge() and persist() (and Hibernate's legacy update()/saveOrUpdate())? Describe what happens step by step when you merge a detached entity, including which instance ends up managed.

Compare the JPA inheritance mapping strategies: SINGLE_TABLE, JOINED and TABLE_PER_CLASS. What are the trade-offs of each in terms of schema, nullability, polymorphic queries and performance, and where does @MappedSuperclass fit in?

When does Hibernate flush the persistence context, and how do FlushModeType.AUTO and COMMIT differ? Why can a JPQL query trigger a flush before it runs, and how does the order of SQL statements at flush time differ from the order of your Java calls?

Explain Hibernate's second-level cache and query cache: what gets stored, how they relate to the first-level cache, the cache concurrency strategies (READ_ONLY, READ_WRITE, etc.), and when enabling them can hurt more than help.

How do you diagnose and fix a slow Hibernate-based application in production? Cover enabling SQL/statistics logging, spotting N+1 and cartesian-product fetch joins, DTO projections instead of entities, read-only queries, and JDBC batching of writes.

questions.question: Which statements about the JPA persistence context (first-level cache) are true?
options: Two find() calls for the same id within one persistence context return the same instance
The first-level cache is shared between all EntityManagers in the application
Changes to a managed entity are written to the database via dirty checking, without calling an update method
The persistence context is cleared automatically after every query

questions.question: Iterating over a list of Orders and calling order.getCustomer() on each one fires an extra SELECT per order (the N+1 problem). Which changes eliminate or substantially reduce the number of queries?
options: Use a JPQL fetch join: SELECT o FROM Order o JOIN FETCH o.customer
Apply an @EntityGraph to the query so customers load together with the orders
Enable batch fetching with @BatchSize so customers are loaded in chunks
Increase the JDBC connection pool size

questions.question: Which statements about optimistic locking with @Version are true?
options: Hibernate appends the version column to the WHERE clause of UPDATE and DELETE statements
A conflict is detected as soon as two transactions read the same row
The @Version field should be incremented manually after each change
@Version acquires a database row lock that is held until the transaction commits
Optimistic locking only works with SERIALIZABLE isolation
